summary: Problem 8 (Duality for Tropical Linear Subspaces) is at an early stage, with only conventions and definitions established before the proof excerpt is truncated. The verifier estimates approximately 10% completeness, meaning the core duality argument has not yet been written or verified. The principal blocker is the absence of any substantive proof body linking the tropical linear subspace to its dual.
problems: The proof attempt is truncated after the setup; no actual duality argument is present, leaving ~90% of the proof unwritten.
No explicit statement or derivation of the duality correspondence (e.g., between a tropical linear subspace and its orthogonal/dual via Plücker or circuit data) is provided.
Definitions and conventions are not yet shown to be sufficient or consistent for the duality claim — verifier could not assess correctness because the substance is missing.
The single in_progress issue lacks a clear roadmap of lemmas needed to bridge definitions to the final duality theorem.
highlights: Conventions and definitions appear to be deliberately and cleanly established, providing a foundation to build on.
The verifier has engaged and produced an explicit completeness estimate (~10%), giving a concrete baseline to track progress.
Scope is well-defined (duality for tropical linear subspaces), enabling targeted next steps.

suggested_todos.title: Write the core duality argument connecting the tropical linear subspace to its dual (Plücker/circuit-vector formulation)
Recover and complete the truncated proof excerpt, ensuring the full body is submitted for verification
Enumerate the intermediate lemmas required (orthogonality, valuated matroid duality, support conditions) and prove each
Confirm the stated definitions and conventions are sufficient and consistent for the duality theorem
Resubmit the completed proof to verifier-agent and target a completeness re-assessment above 80%
